title: Klarna API credentials should not be disclosed
description: Klarna is a buy-now-pay-later (BNPL) and payment processing platform used globally for e-commerce. Klarna API credentials include username/password pairs for the Payments API and OAuth tokens. Exposing these enables unauthorized payment session creation, order manipulation, and access to customer purchase data.
recommendation: Remove Klarna credentials from source code. Store username and password in environment variables. Rotate immediately in the Klarna Merchant Portal.
impact: Unauthorized payment session creation, order management, customer purchase data access, and PCI-DSS compliance violation.

checks.message: Klarna API password/secret detected in variable assignment. Klarna API credentials authenticate payment sessions for buy-now-pay-later transactions. Exposing these credentials allows creating fraudulent payment sessions, capturing and canceling orders, accessing customer purchase history and personal data, and generating unauthorized refunds. This is a PCI-DSS compliance violation. Rotate immediately in the Klarna Merchant Portal.

Klarna API username detected. Klarna uses username/password authentication for their Payments API. A username alone is less sensitive but is typically found alongside the password in the same configuration. Ensure the Klarna API password is not hardcoded in the same file.
